draft.introduction: The rich ruler asks Jesus what he must do to inherit eternal life. Jesus’ response—“sell what you have”—and the saying about camels and needles provoked centuries of debate on wealth and salvation.

translation.text: And as he was setting out on his journey, a man ran up and knelt before him and asked him, “Good Teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?” And Jesus said to him, “Why do you call me good? No one is good except God alone.” And Jesus, looking at him, loved him, and said, “You lack one thing: go, sell all that you have and give to the poor.” And Jesus said to his disciples, “How difficult it will be for those who have wealth to enter the kingdom of God! It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle.”
translation.original_text: τί με λέγεις ἀγαθόν οὐδεὶς ἀγαθὸς εἰ μὴ εἷς ὁ θεός

translator_notes.note: “No one is good except God alone” (οὐδεὶς ἀγαθὸς εἰ μὴ εἷς ὁ θεός) was used by Arius to argue Jesus denied his own divinity. The orthodox response: Jesus is not denying his goodness but probing the man’s understanding of what “good” means.
